Problem: Running Etch rate qual slim46: Alarm RF1 reflected power out of compliance (Limit=15.0W, Actual=33.0W). In Slot1, 2 out of 5 monitors for N2O2-100S recipe show no etching from last night.
Actions: Adjusted RF1 Reflected Tolerance: originally 100W, previously reduced to 6W to force no-plasma alarms, but this caused issues for recipes using high RF1 power (Slim46 uses 100W). Reset tolerance to 30W to allow step #7 to complete without shutting RF1 output while keeping tighter limit than 100W. For second issue, ran recipe N2O2-100S with 2 dummy wafers and in step #8 detuned RF1 until RF1 VDC dropped just below 50V to confirm tool alarms and aborts; observed that even at RF1 VDC 50/40/30V there should be some etch, so zero etch is considered impossible and escalated for help. Noted open question about when step #8 70V VDC monitoring occurs (before or after previous night). Left RF1 reflected tolerance decision (keep at 30W or revert to 100W) to ME and management. Completed testing with 10 dummy wafers alternating between EtchRate and N2O2-100S recipes without any further issues.
Category: RF voltage is out of compliance

COMMENT: *Need to place the rf generator on the, rf dummy load and operate the, generator at full power for 1 hour, to verify the generator is good., *If the rf generator is good, need to, operate the failing process and note, the "load" and "tune" rf capacitor, movements before and after the, failure and place the load and tune, rf capacitor movements in mesa., *If the load and tune rf capacitor, movements are abnormal, need to, observe rf reflected power., *If rf reflected power is present,, need to trouble shoot the rf reflected, power problem.
Problem: Possible RF generator issue or RF matching problem indicated by abnormal load/tune capacitor movements and presence of reflected power during process failure.
Actions: Place the RF generator on the RF dummy load and operate at full power for 1 hour to verify the generator is good. If the RF generator is good, operate the failing process and note the load and tune RF capacitor movements before and after the failure, then place the movements in MESA. If the load and tune RF capacitor movements are abnormal, observe RF reflected power. If RF reflected power is present, troubleshoot the RF reflected power problem.
